source.module: Core Django Fundamentals – Templates & Static Content

topicTags: Core Django Fundamentals – Templates & Static Content

content.problemStatement:
In Django, relative URLs are a way to define links within your project without specifying the full path. This approach is useful especially when you're working on larger projects.

When we covered URL patterns in Django, we learned how to define URL routes using the urlpatterns list in the urls.py file. \
Relative URLs build upon this concept by allowing you to reference other views or pages within your project using shorter, more convenient paths.

To use relative URLs in your Django project, you can use the **{% url %}** template tag in your HTML templates. \
Suppose your urlpattern in `urls.py` is defined as follows.
```python
urlpatterns = [
    path('about/', views.about, name='view_name'),
]
```
Instead of hardcoding URLs, you can use the name you gave to your URL pattern in your HTML file.
```html

Link


Link
```

Relative URL makes your links automatically update if the URL pattern changes — you don’t need to manually change the link everywhere. We will see this in action in the worked example covered next.
